back_translation: When Jesus went back into the city of Capernaum again after a few days had passed, there were people who heard that He was at home.
A large number of people gathered together until there was no space left, even the area in front of the door had none, and He proclaimed the Word to them.
People brought a paralyzed man to Him, with four men carrying him.

When they could not get in to Him because of the crowd, they took apart the roof at the place where He was staying, and having dug an opening, they lowered down the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
When Jesus beheld their faith, He said to the paralyzed man: 'My child, your sins have received forgiveness.'
Some of the scribes were sitting there and thinking in their own hearts:
'Why does this man speak like this? He is dishonoring God! Who can forgive sins, except God alone?'
At that moment, Jesus perceived in His spirit that they were thinking that way within themselves, so He said to them: 'Why are you thinking these things in your hearts?'
'Which is easier — to say to the paralyzed man, "Your sins have received forgiveness," or to say, "Rise up, take up your mat, and walk"?'
'But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority in the world to forgive sins' — then He said to the paralyzed man:
'I command you: Rise up, take up your mat, and go back home.'
He rose up, and immediately took up the mat and walked out in front of everyone, so that all were stunned and praised God, saying: 'We have never seen anything like this.'
He went out to the seaside again; the whole crowd came to Him, and He taught them.
As He was walking along, He beheld Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax station, and He said to him: 'Come follow me.' Levi rose up and followed Him.
While He was reclining to eat in Levi's house, many tax collectors and sinners shared the table with Jesus and His disciples, for there were many who followed Him.
When the scribes of the Pharisee faction saw that He was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they said to His disciples: 'Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?'
Jesus heard and said to them: 'Those who are well do not need a doctor, but the sick do. I have not come to call the righteous, but to call sinners.'

At that time, the disciples of John and the Pharisees were fasting. So some people came to ask Him: 'Why do the disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?'
Jesus answered them: 'Can the friends of the bridegroom fast while the bridegroom is still with them? As long as the bridegroom is with them, they cannot fast.'
'But a day will come when the bridegroom will be torn away from them, and on that day they will fast.'
'No one takes unshrunk cloth to patch an old garment. If one does so, the new cloth will pull the old cloth and tear it, and the tear will become even worse.'

'No one pours new wine into old leather bags. If one does so, the wine will cause the leather bags to tear, and both the wine and the leather bags will be ruined together. But new wine must be poured into new leather bags.'
On one Sabbath day, while He was passing through the grainfields, His disciples began to walk along while picking the heads of grain as they went.
The Pharisees said to Him: 'Look! Why are they doing what is against the law on the Sabbath?'
He said to them: 'Have you never read what David did, when he and those with him were in need and hungry?'

'He entered the house of God in the time when Abiathar was the high priest, and ate the bread of the presence, which is not permitted for anyone to eat except the priests alone, and he also gave it to those who were with him to eat.'
He went on to say: 'The Sabbath was created for man, not man created for the Sabbath.'
'Therefore the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath also.'
